text:

website running and free for all - thank you!

The Dutch Royal Family has welcomed Olympic royalty to Huis ten
Bosch Palace to celebrate their incredible
accomplishments.

King Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima hosted all of the Dutch
athletes who won medals at the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris at a
reception in Paris on Tuesday.

The Netherlands had a very successful Olympic Games in Paris,
winning a record number of medals. Athletes won thirty four medals
total: they earned fifteen gold medals, seven silver medals, and
twelve bronze medals to place sixth on the final medal table. The
country sent their largest contingent ever, with 273 athletes
competing for the Netherlands.

The athletes were first honoured at a ceremony in the Glass Hall
at The Hague where the government celebrated their Olympic
victories.

The athletes then headed to the palace to meet King
Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima.

The Dutch king gave a heartfelt speech where he thanked athletes
for all of their hard work. Both the King and the Queen of the
Netherlands chatted with athletes for quite some time.

King Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima also took a historic
photo with all of the Dutch Olympic medalists outside of Huis ten
Bosch Palace, complete with their gold, silver, and bronze medals
and big smiles.

title: Dutch King and Queen welcome Olympic medallists home - Royal Central
description: The Dutch Royal Family has welcomed Olympic royalty to Huis ten Bosch Palace to celebrate their incredible accomplishments.
